type: B2B hospitality SaaS — QR/app at-table ordering & order management for restaurants/bars (web + iOS/Android), UK
one_line_reason: Right shape (a real UK web+mobile hospitality SaaS you could operate) but the growth lever is B2B restaurant/bar sales — your weakness — in a brutally saturated market owned by POS incumbents (Toast/Square/SpotOn) and specialists (Sunday, me&u); it's order-management-only (no payments), shows no disclosed traction, and is just over budget. growth_lever_fits = false → PASS.

operability_upside.note: Real web + native mobile SaaS on your stack — you can run/maintain it. But it's a mature commodity: little product you could add would move the needle; the bottleneck is sales, not features.

pmf_retention.note: No customers/revenue disclosed; 'domain + software for $4k' phrasing implies ~zero traction. Restaurant tools also churn hard. Unverified — key unknown.

unit_economics.note: Model is fine ($49.99/mo or $399.99/yr per venue, recurring) — but current revenue is unknown and almost certainly negligible.

moat_defensibility.note: Near-zero moat. Commodity QR ordering; NO payments (it rides your existing till), so it loses to POS-bundled QR from Toast/Square/SpotOn and to Sunday/me&u. 'A lot of QR menus are just a PDF + sticker.'

growth_market.note: Digital ordering is growing, but this segment is saturated and owned by POS incumbents; a standalone no-payments tool is on the wrong side of that.

tech_ip.note: Real transferable asset (web + iOS + Android apps + backend + domain, UK Ltd) — more than a content site. But native apps mean ongoing maintenance + App Store/Play account transfer to confirm.

transfer_key_person.note: Assets (code/domain/apps) are impersonal and transfer — but with ~no customer base there's little growth engine to inherit either way.

deal_terms.note: $4,000 for a no-revenue commodity codebase a team like yours could rebuild — poor value — and it's over the $3k budget (1.3x).

seller_adverse_selection.note: 'Domain + software for sale', no revenue mentioned = built-but-couldn't-sell in a hard market. Not fraud; just likely no traction.

key_unknowns: Any paying venues / MRR at all? (couldn't read the Reddit body — my fetch tool is blocked from reddit.com). If there's real, growing MRR with a repeatable channel it changes the picture; the phrasing suggests not.
Why selling, and what's included beyond domain + code (the iOS/Android app-store listings + accounts? the RoomDrop sibling? the TableDrop LTD entity?).
Is there ANY non-sales acquisition channel (a POS partnership, a directory, inbound) — or is it 100% cold B2B hospitality sales?
Code/IP ownership + clean transfer of the App Store / Google Play developer accounts.
Ongoing costs (hosting, app-store fees, any third-party APIs).
seller_questions: How many paying venues are live today, MRR, and churn — screen-shared from Stripe/billing? (PMF + revenue; the decisive unknown)
Why are you selling, and how did you acquire the venues you have (cold sales, a POS partner, inbound)? (channel — does any non-sales lever exist?)
Exactly what's included: domain, full codebase, the iOS + Android apps AND their developer accounts, the RoomDrop sibling, the TableDrop LTD company? (IP/transfer)
Is 100% of the code and design owned by you (no license-limited templates), and will the app-store accounts transfer? (IP)
Monthly running costs and any third-party dependencies? (unit economics)
Any existing POS/payment integrations or partnerships that would help distribution? (moat/channel)
inputs_provided: Reddit r/saasforsale listing 'TableDrop.com – Domain and Software for sale ($4000)' [title provided by user; POST BODY NOT READ — reddit.com is on the fetch blocklist, so revenue/why-selling are UNKNOWN pending the user pasting them]

Product site: QR/app at-table ordering & order management for restaurants/bars; free customer app + QR; geolocation menu; order dashboard; works alongside existing payment/till systems (NOT payments); UK 'TableDrop LTD'; sibling product RoomDrop [VERIFIED via tabledrop.com]
Pricing: $49.99/mo or $399.99/yr per venue, 7-day trial, no contract [VERIFIED via tabledrop.com/pricing]

Market: QR at-table ordering is saturated/commoditized in 2026 — POS incumbents (Toast, Square, SpotOn, Eats365) bundle it; specialists (Sunday 3,500+ clients/80M+ tx/yr, me&u, TableQR, UpMenu) compete [VERIFIED via market research]
Asking $4,000 vs the team's $3,000 budget → affordability gate: over budget (suppressed in the verdict because merit is already PASS)
